steps.title: Translate the relationship into an equation
steps.content:
Let $n$ be the smaller integer. The larger integer is $5$ greater than twice the smaller, so it equals $2n+5$. Their product is $462$:
$$n(2n+5)=462$$

steps.title: Solve the quadratic
steps.content:
Expand and rearrange to standard form:
$$2n^2+5n-462=0$$
Apply the quadratic formula with $a=2$, $b=5$, $c=-462$:
$$n=\frac{-5\pm\sqrt{25+4(2)(462)}}{4}=\frac{-5\pm\sqrt{3721}}{4}=\frac{-5\pm 61}{4}$$
The positive root is $n=\frac{56}{4}=14$.

steps.title: Verify
steps.content:
If $n=14$, the larger integer is $2(14)+5=33$, and $14\times 33=462$, which matches.
The smaller integer is 14.
